title: 10 medicinal constituents of Alisma plantago-aquatica (water plantain)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Alisma plantago-aquatica asiasca is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Alismataceae. It is a perennial herbaceous plant that grows in shallow waters and muddy or sandy areas. The plant has long, narrow leaves with wavy edges and produces small, yellowish-green flowers on stalks above the water surface.

Alisma plantago-aquatica lycorin is a bioactive compound extracted from the rhizomes of the plant. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Lycorin has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immune-boosting activities, making it a potential therapeutic agent for the treatment of various diseases, including cancer and cardiovascular disorders.

Alisma plantago-aquatica alismatisaponin A is a saponin compound extracted from the roots of Alisma plantago-aquatica. This compound has been extensively studied for its potential therapeutic applications, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Additionally, alismatisaponin A has been found to exhibit antioxidant activity and may be effective in treating various diseases such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and dermatological disorders.

Alisma plantago-aquatica triterpence acids is a type of compound extracted from the leaves and stems of this aquatic plant. These compounds have been found to possess various bioactivities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Triterpenoids are a class of natural products that have been widely studied for their potential therapeutic applications in traditional medicine and modern healthcare.

Alisma plantago-aquatica oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid compound extracted from the roots of this aquatic plant. It has been extensively studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Oleanolic acid has been shown to exhibit selective cytotoxicity against cancer cells, making it a promising candidate for the development of new anticancer agents.

Alisma plantago-aquatica ursolic acid is a type of triterpenoid saponin that has been isolated from the roots of this aquatic plant. This compound has been found to exhibit various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antitumor, and antioxidant properties. Ursolic acid has also been shown to inhibit the growth of certain cancer cells and may have potential therapeutic applications in the prevention and treatment of diseases such as cardiovascular disease and Alzheimer's disease.

Alisma plantago-aquatica flavonoids (e.g., quercetin) is a group of bioactive compounds found in its leaves and stems. These flavonoids have been extensively studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to the plant's traditional uses in folk medicine. Quercetin, in particular, has been shown to possess strong free radical scavenging activity and may help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage.

Alisma plantago-aquatica triterpenoid saponins is a type of bioactive compound derived from the rhizomes and leaves of the plant. These saponins have been shown to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. They have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a range of diseases, including respiratory infections and skin conditions, and are also being investigated for their potential use as natural antimicrobial agents.

Alisma plantago-aquatica sterols (e.g., β-sitosterol) is a group of compounds extracted from the rhizomes of this aquatic plant. These sterols have been shown to possess various bioactivities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Alisma plantago-aquatica has been used to treat a range of ailments, including edema, cough, and diarrhea. The sterols isolated from this plant may contribute to its therapeutic properties.

Alisma plantago-aquatica polyacetylene compounds is a group of bioactive molecules that have been isolated from this aquatic plant. These compounds have been found to possess a range of pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. They have also been shown to exhibit antioxidant activity and may play a role in the prevention of certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.

intro: Alisma plantago-aquatica has active constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. These constituents contribute to the plant's medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic activities. The flavonoids can help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, while the saponins may aid in lowering blood sugar levels. By harnessing these beneficial compounds, water plantain has been traditionally used to treat a range of conditions, from digestive issues to skin irritations, ultimately improving overall health and well-being for individuals who utilize it.
intro_desc: Alisma plantago-aquatica has active constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These constituents are responsible for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The diuretic effect can help alleviate edema and urinary disorders, while anti-inflammatory properties can soothe digestive issues. For example, water plantain has been used to treat hypertension, kidney stones, and skin conditions, improving the quality of life for individuals with these conditions. Its antioxidant properties also contribute to its overall health benefits.
